description: This figure presents Mössbauer transmission spectra (left column) and the corresponding hyperfine magnetic field distributions P(Bhf) (right column) for α-Fe foil and ferritic low-density steels with increasing aluminum contents: Fe-5Al, Fe-6Al, Fe-6.8Al, and Fe-9Al. Panels (a, c, e, g, i) show velocity-dependent transmission spectra fitted with theoretical sextets (black lines) to experimental data (red markers), while panels (b, d, f, h, j) display the probability density of the hyperfine field Bhf, where the humps are deconvoluted into individual Gaussian-like components (green curves). With rising Al addition, the spectra progressively broaden and lose the symmetric six-line profile characteristic of α-Fe, and the Bhf distributions evolve from a dominant single peak near 33 T toward increasingly complex multipeak profiles with additional low-field components between approximately 20 T and 32 T. This systematic change reflects altered local atomic environments around Fe atoms and indicates the development of short-range order or nano-sized ordered phases, providing spectroscopic evidence for local structure evolution underlying the strengthening behavior in these steels.
